---
title: "Mermaid Masterclass: Power User Guide"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    css: style.css
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
    mermaid-format: svg
    mermaid:
      theme: dark
---

# 🧜‍♂️ Mermaid Masterclass: Power User Guide

> **Objective:** Reduce communication noise between agents and humans by utilizing high-fidelity, version-controlled diagrams.

---

## 🏗️ The 2026 Mermaid Directory

Mermaid.js has expanded significantly. Choosing the correct diagram type is the first step to reducing noise.

### 🌓 Flowcharts: Logic & Hierarchy
Flowcharts are the workhorse of documentation. For power users, the key is **Styling** and **Interactive Nodes**.

```{mermaid}
graph TB
    subgraph "External Ingestion"
        API[API Endpoint] -- Payload --> VAL{Validator}
    end

    subgraph "Internal Processing"
        VAL -- Pass --> REF(Refinery Engine)
        VAL -- Fail --> DLO((Dead Letter))
    end

    REF --> REG[Master Registry]
    
    %% Advanced Styling
    classDef highPrio fill:#10a37f,stroke:#fff,stroke-width:2px,color:#fff;
    classDef storage fill:#333,stroke:#10a37f,stroke-width:1px;
    class REF highPrio;
    class REG storage;
```

### 📡 Sequence Diagrams: Communication Design
Crucial for API design. Use `par` for asynchronous operations and `critical` for error handling.

```{mermaid}
sequenceDiagram
    autonumber
    actor U as User
    participant A as Agent
    participant C as Collider
    participant G as GCS

    U->>A: "Analyze Project"
    A->>C: trigger_pipeline()
    
    par Async Processing
        C->>G: sync_mirror()
        C->>G: upload_parcels()
    end

    alt Success
        G-->>C: 200 OK
        C-->>A: analysis_complete
        A-->>U: "Project Mapped"
    else Failure
        G-->>C: 500 Error
        C-->>A: retry_failed
        critical Manual Intervention
            A->>U: "Requesting API Key"
        end
    end
```

### 📊 Gantt Charts: The Timeline of Truth
Use Gantt charts to visualize the **Consolidation Roadmap** and dependencies.

```{mermaid}
gantt
    title Project Elements: Consolidation Roadmap
    dateFormat  YYYY-MM-DD
    section Infrastructure
    Verification Suite      :done,    des1, 2026-01-20, 2026-01-25
    Consolidation Dispatcher:done,    des2, 2026-01-25, 2026-01-27
    section Execution
    Archive Pass 1 (Links)  :active,  des3, 2026-01-28, 3d
    Archive Pass 2 (Fixes)  :         des4, after des3, 5d
    Root Clean-up           :         des5, 2026-02-01, 2d
```

### 🧬 Entity Relationship (ER): The Node Schema
Defines the "Intimate Properties" of our data parcels.

```{mermaid}
erDiagram
    PROJECT ||--o{ SUBSYSTEM : "contains"
    SUBSYSTEM ||--o{ FILE : "manages"
    FILE ||--o{ PARCEL : "refines into"
    
    PARCEL {
        string id PK
        string waybill_id FK
        string content_hash
        int token_balance
    }
```

### 🌀 State Diagrams: The Finite Logic
Perfect for documenting the lifecycle of a **Waybill** or a **Refinery Job**.

```{mermaid}
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Ingested
    Ingested --> Validating
    Validating --> Processing: Valid
    Validating --> Failed: Invalid
    Processing --> Refined: Success
    Processing --> Retrying: Temporary Error
    Retrying --> Processing
    Refined --> [*]
    Failed --> [*]
```

### 🧩 Other Specialized Charts

#### 🌳 Mindmaps: Domain Discovery
Ideal for mapping the **Hemispheres** of the project without strict hierarchy.

```{mermaid}
mindmap
  root((Project Elements))
    Particle(The Body)
        Collider Engine
        Theory Canon
        Schemas
    Wave(The Brain)
        Refinery
        ACI
        Parcels
    Logistics(The Flow)
        Couriers
        Dispatchers
        Waybills
    Observer(The Law)
        Governance
        Socratic Audit
        Quality Gates
```

#### 🌿 Git Graphs: Branching Strategy
Visualize the **Consolidation Loop** development.

```{mermaid}
gitGraph
    commit id: "INITIAL"
    branch develop
    checkout develop
    commit id: "refinery-v1"
    commit id: "dispatcher-v1"
    branch fix/analyze-bug
    checkout fix/analyze-bug
    commit id: "patch-scoping"
    checkout develop
    merge fix/analyze-bug
    checkout main
    merge develop tag: "v1.0-consolidated"
```

#### 🌊 Sankey: Data Volume Logistics
Track how raw context flows and scales through the platform.

```{mermaid}
sankey-beta
    Ingestion,Refinement,1000
    Refinement,GraphStore,800
    Refinement,DeadLetter,200
    GraphStore,Intelligence,750
    GraphStore,Archive,50
```

#### 🗺️ Timeline: Project Evolution
Track the history of the **Standard Model of Code**.

```{mermaid}
timeline
    title Project Elements History
    2024 : Initial Particle Discovery : Theory Foundation
    2025 : Collider V1 : Unified Analysis : Legacy Accumulation
    2026 : The Great Consolidation : Refinery Launch : Symmetry Protocol
```

#### 📐 Quadrant: Prioritization Matrix
Map tasks by **Impact** vs **Complexity**.

```{mermaid}
quadrantChart
    title Task Prioritization (Q1 2026)
    x-axis Low Impact --> High Impact
    y-axis High Complexity --> Low Complexity
    quadrant-1 Quick Wins
    quadrant-2 Strategic Projects
    quadrant-3 Minor Fixes
    quadrant-4 Research/Waste
    Link Fixer: [0.8, 0.9]
    Refinery V2: [0.9, 0.3]
    Doc Sync: [0.4, 0.7]
    Link Fixer: [0.8, 0.9]
    Refinery V2: [0.9, 0.3]
    Doc Sync: [0.4, 0.7]
    Legacy Removal: [0.6, 0.2]
```

#### 📋 Requirement Diagrams: Quality Gates
Track system requirements and verification status.

```{mermaid}
requirementDiagram

    requirement project_elements_v1 {
        id: 1
        text: System must achieve 50% drift reduction.
        risk: medium
        verifymethod: test
    }

    element drift_verifier {
        type: script
    }

    drift_verifier - satisfies -> project_elements_v1
```

#### 🥧 Pie Charts: Corpus Analysis
Visualize the distribution of repository hemispheres.

```{mermaid}
pie title Corpus Distribution (2026)
    "Body (Particle)" : 45
    "Brain (Wave)" : 30
    "Observer" : 15
    "Logistics" : 10
```

#### 🚶 User Journeys: CLI Interaction
Map the developer experience using the `pe` CLI.

```{mermaid}
journey
    title Developer Consolidation Flow
    section Setup
      Sync Mirror: 5: Developer
      Target Registry: 3: Developer
    section Consolidation
      Run Link Fixer: 5: Agent, Developer
      Manual Verification: 4: Developer
    section Finalize
      Commit Changes: 5: Developer
```

---

## ⚡ Power User Best Practices

### 1. The "Single Screen" Rule
If your diagram requires vertical scrolling, it has too much **Noise**. 
- **Solution:** Break one large flowchart into multiple sub-flowcharts linked via identifiers.

### 2. Standardized Color Palette (ChatGPT Aesthetic)
Stick to our established `style.css` colors for consistency:
- **Primary Green (#10a37f)**: Actionable nodes, start points, success states.
- **Dark Neutral (#171717)**: Backgrounds and container borders.
- **Vibrant Red (#ff4b4b)**: Failure states and blockers.

### 3. Click Events & Tooltips
Merge these into your documentation for interactivity.

```markdown
# In your QMD render:
click REF "file:///path/to/refinery.py" "Open Source Code"
```

---

## 🔇 Reducing Communication Noise

In a collaborative environment (Agent ↔ Human), diagrams act as a **Sync Pulse**.

1.  **Define Inputs/Outputs first**: Use an ER diagram to agree on the schema before writing a single line of code.
2.  **Verify Flow**: Use a Sequence diagram to confirm "who talks to whom" across hemispheres.
3.  **Audit History**: Use Git-committed Mermaid files (like this one) to track how architecture evolved.

---

> [!TIP]
> **Pro Tip**: Use the `%%{init: {'theme': 'dark'}}%%` directive at the top of a chart to override global styles for specific visualizations.

---

## 🏗️ Advanced: C4 Architecture Context
For massive scale architecture, use the C4 model abstraction in Mermaid.

```{mermaid}
graph TB
    subgraph "External Systems"
        GCS[Google Cloud Storage]
        Neo4j[Graph Store]
    end

    subgraph "PROJECT_elements"
        PE_CLI[pe CLI] --> PE_CORE[Core Engine]
        PE_CORE --> Refinery[Context Refinery]
        PE_CORE --> Collider[Symmetry Collider]
    end

    PE_CLI --> GCS
    Refinery --> GCS
    Collider --> Neo4j
```
